antipattern.description: ORM filtering / writes / external API calls inside the view body.
antipattern.reason: Views become untestable in isolation. Business logic locked to HTTP transport — cannot be reused in tasks or management commands.

antipattern.description: Service signature `def create_order(request, ...)` accepting the Request object.
antipattern.reason: Service unusable outside views (no request in a Celery task). Couples business logic to HTTP forever.

antipattern.description: openapi.yaml maintained by hand alongside the code.
antipattern.reason: Drifts by the second sprint. Consumers integrate against the spec, then break when reality differs.

antipattern.description: ListAPIView returning full queryset with no pagination.
antipattern.reason: First customer with 10k rows DoS's the endpoint.

antipattern.description: Custom error JSON in some endpoints, default DRF error in others.
antipattern.reason: Consumer teams write per-endpoint error handlers. Trust in the API erodes.

antipattern.description: Serializer methods perform DB writes or call external APIs.
antipattern.reason: Serializers become services with extra steps; testing requires constructing fake DRF context. Move writes to services.
